title: Reigate Hill Fortress Memorial
memorial_type: Memorial sculpture by Surrey artist Roger Day in woodland clearing

maintainer.notes: Memorial sculpture by Surrey artist Roger Day in woodland clearing on National Trust land.

external_narratives.summary: National Trust page about the B-17G crash on Reigate Hill on 19 March 1945, killing nine USAAF airmen from the 384th Bombardment Group. The crew were returning from bombing an oil refinery at Plauen, Germany, when their battle-damaged aircraft entered cloud and struck trees.
key_details: Returning from Plauen oil refinery raid

external_narratives.summary: Details of the 'Wing Tips' memorial sculpture by Surrey artist Roger Day. The two oak pieces are spaced exactly 32 metres (105 feet) apart, matching the B-17 wingspan. Molten metal recovered from the crash site is embedded within the sculpture.
key_details: Pieces spaced at exact B-17 wingspan (32m/105ft)
Contains molten metal from crash site

external_narratives.summary: Coverage of the 80th anniversary commemoration in 2025, attended by the US Air Attache and civic leaders. The event marked continued Anglo-American remembrance of the nine crew members.
IWM War Memorials Register entry for the 384th Bomb Group B-17 Flying Fortress 43-39035 memorial on Reigate Hill. National-level record of the memorial.

US War Memorials registry listing crew names: pilot Robert S. Griffin, co-pilot Herbert S. Geller, navigator Royal A. Runyon, and six enlisted men including Robert Manbeck, Donald W. Jeffrey, Philip J. Phillips Jr, Robert F. Marshall, William R. Irons, and Thomas J. Hickey.
Wings Aviation Museum page dedicated to B-17G 43-39035 and its crew, providing detailed biographical information and crash context within the museum's aircrew memorials collection.
key_details: Part of Wings Aviation Museum aircrew memorials collection

sources: Photon OSM — Reigate Hill Surrey named feature at 51.255 -0.200
location_confidence.verification_notes: CORRECTION. Current coords (51.068, -0.476) ~20km south/28km west of Reigate Hill. OSM named hill at 51.255, -0.200.
